ucs.title: [LLM] FIN8 (Syssphinx) application-shim persistence via sdbinst.exe loading a non-Microsoft .sdb
ucs.description: Hunts FIN8's signature persistence: registering a custom Application Compatibility Shim Database (.sdb) with sdbinst.exe to hijack execution of legitimate binaries. Mandiant/Bitdefender reporting on FIN8's BADHATCH and Sardonic intrusions repeatedly observed sdbinst loading attacker-authored .sdb files dropped to %TEMP% / %ProgramData% — a TTP almost no other major financially-motivated crew uses at scale.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="sdbinst.exe" Processes.process IN ("*-q*","*-p*","*-u*") (Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*.sdb*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as sdb_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as sdb_file from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.sdb" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | where isnotnull(sdb_path) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let sdb_drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".sdb"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","setup.exe","wusa.exe")
    | project SdbTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, SdbPath = FolderPath, SdbName = FileName, Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, DropperSha = InitiatingProcessSHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName =~ "sdbinst.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-q","-p","-u",".sdb")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\AppData\")
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmiprvse.exe","rundll32.exe")
| join kind=inner sdb_drops on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (SdbTime .. SdbTime + 10m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          SdbName, SdbPath, Dropper, DropperCmd, DropperSha
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let sdb_drops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".sdb"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\")
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","trustedinstaller.exe","setup.exe","wusa.exe")
    | project SdbTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SdbPath = TargetFilePath, SdbName = TargetFileName, Dropper = ActingProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\sdbinst.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("-q","-p","-u",".sdb")
| where CommandLine has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\", @"\AppData\")
   or ParentProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmiprvse.exe","rundll32.exe")
| join kind=inner (sdb_drops) on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (SdbTime .. SdbTime + 10m)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, SdbName, SdbPath, Dropper
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: FIN8 Application Shim Database Installation from Suspicious Path
id: 6a2c1f9d-3a4b-4c1d-8b5e-fin8sdbinst01
status: experimental
description: Detects sdbinst.exe registering a .sdb file from a user-writable path, the persistence technique used by FIN8 (Syssphinx) to deploy BADHATCH/Sardonic.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0061/
  - https://www.bitdefender.com/files/News/CaseStudies/study/394/Bitdefender-PR-Whitepaper-FIN8-creat5619-en-EN.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1546.011
  - attack.g0061
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\sdbinst.exe'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '.sdb'
      - ' -q '
      - ' -p '
  selection_path:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\'
  filter_legit_parents:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\wusa.exe'
  condition: selection_image and selection_args and selection_path and not filter_legit_parents
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate application compatibility shim deployment by IT (rare; should be packaged through MSI/Intune)
level: high
ucs.rationale: FIN8 is one of the only financially-motivated actors that has consistently weaponised Application Shimming (.sdb registration via sdbinst.exe) for persistence — first documented in their PUNCHBUGGY/BADHATCH operations and reused across the Sardonic intrusions tracked by Bitdefender and Symantec. Pairing the sdbinst execution with a same-host .sdb drop in user-writable paths within a 10-minute wind
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN8 Sardonic loader chain — Office macro spawns obfuscated PowerShell that invokes WMI Win32_Process for lateral execution
ucs.description: Targets FIN8's repeatable post-phishing chain: malicious Office document spawns highly-obfuscated PowerShell (heavy `^` / backtick / format-string evasion characteristic of FIN8 reported by FireEye 2017 and Symantec 2023), which then uses WMI (`Win32_Process.Create` against a remote node) to stage the Sardonic backdoor. The combination of macro→PowerShell→remote-WMI in one process tree is a FIN8 fingerprint, distinct from FIN7 (which prefers JavaScript/MSHTA) and most ransomware affiliates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_id as ps_pid, process as ps_cmdline | eval obf_score=case(match(ps_cmdline,"(?i)\^|`|\$\{[a-z]\}|-[Ee][NnCc]|FromBase64String|::([A-Z][a-z]+)\(['\"][a-z]\{0,2\}['\"]"),3,match(ps_cmdline,"(?i)IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|hidden|bypass"),2,1==1,0) | where obf_score>=2 | join type=inner dest ps_pid [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as wmic_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" OR Processes.process_name="WmiPrvSE.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe") (Processes.process="*Win32_Process*" OR Processes.process="*/node:*" OR Processes.process="*-ComputerName*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WmiMethod*" OR Processes.process="*Get-WmiObject*Create*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_id as ps_pid] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ObfPS = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (office_parents)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
    | where cmd matches regex @"(\^|`|\$\{[a-z]\}|-e[nc]+ |frombase64string|::(\w+)\(['\x22][a-z]{0,3}['\x22])"
       or cmd has_any ("hidden","-nop","bypass","downloadstring","iex","invoke-expression")
    | project PsTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              PsPid = ProcessId, PsCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              MacroParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, MacroParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("process call create","/node:","win32_process"))
   or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
       and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-WmiMethod","Get-WmiObject","Win32_Process","-ComputerName"))
   or (FileName =~ "WmiPrvSE.exe")
| join kind=inner ObfPS on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.PsPid
| where Timestamp between (PsTime .. PsTime + 10m)
| project DeviceName, AccountName, MacroTime = PsTime,
          MacroParent, PsCmd,
          WmiTime = Timestamp, WmiBin = FileName, WmiCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by MacroTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let ObfPS = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | extend NewBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentBin in (office_parents)
    | where NewBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
    | where cmd matches regex @"(\^|`|\$\{[a-z]\}|-e[nc]+ |frombase64string|::(\w+)\(['\x22][a-z]{0,3}['\x22])"
       or cmd has_any ("hidden","-nop","bypass","downloadstring","iex","invoke-expression")
    | project PsTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, PsPid = NewProcessId, PsCmd = CommandLine, MacroParent = ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend NewBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where (NewBin == "wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("process call create","/node:","Win32_Process"))
   or (NewBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("Invoke-WmiMethod","Get-WmiObject","Win32_Process","-ComputerName"))
   or (NewBin == "wmiprvse.exe")
| join kind=inner (ObfPS) on Computer, $left.ProcessId == $right.PsPid
| where TimeGenerated between (PsTime .. PsTime + 10m)
| project Computer, Account, MacroTime = PsTime, MacroParent, PsCmd,
          WmiTime = TimeGenerated, WmiBin = NewProcessName, WmiCmd = CommandLine
| order by MacroTime desc
ucs.rationale: FIN8's tradecraft (FireEye 2017 obfuscation paper, Bitdefender 2021 Sardonic, Symantec 2023 ALPHV-Sardonic) consistently chains Office macro → heavily token-obfuscated PowerShell (caret/backtick/format-string substitution) → WMI Win32_Process for moving Sardonic to a peer host. Few other crews tie all three together in one process tree: FIN7 prefers MSHTA/JS, Conti affiliates lean on Cobalt Strike
